vata.principle: Gentle, grounding, rhythmic. Exercise to 50% capacity. Avoid exhaustion. Warmth and regularity matter.
vata.bestTiming: Morning (7-8 AM) or early evening. Avoid late night exercise.
vata.capacityRule: Stop when you start breathing through your mouth or sweating on forehead. Vata should never feel drained after exercise.

recommended.details: Grounding poses: Tadasana (Mountain), Virabhadrasana (Warrior), Vrksasana (Tree), Balasana (Child's), Paschimottanasana (Seated Forward Bend), Savasana (extended). Slow, steady flow. Hold poses longer.
Gentle, rhythmic walking in nature. Flat terrain preferred. Avoid windy or cold conditions.
Slow, flowing movements. Excellent for Vata's nervous system. Grounding and calming.
Gentle laps in a heated pool. The water supports Vata's joints. Avoid cold water.
Slow, rhythmic dance like ballroom, classical Indian dance. Grounds the body while being creative.
Easy, flat terrain. Gentle pace. Indoor cycling in cold weather.
Slow, supported stretches. Focus on joints and flexibility without strain.

avoid: Any exercise that leaves you exhausted

pitta.principle: Moderate, cooling, non-competitive. Exercise to 70% capacity. Avoid overheating and aggression. Fun over performance.
pitta.bestTiming: Early morning (6-8 AM) or evening (after 6 PM). NEVER exercise in midday heat.
pitta.capacityRule: Stop when perspiration appears on nose, forehead, and armpits. Pitta should feel pleasantly worked, not overheated or aggressive.

recommended.details: THE best exercise for Pitta. Cooling, full-body, non-competitive. Lakes, pools, ocean — all excellent.
Chandra Namaskar, forward bends, twists, gentle inversions. Avoid hot yoga. Focus on surrender and letting go rather than achievement.
Moderate pace. Scenic routes in nature. Early morning or evening. Enjoy the journey, not the speed.
Moderate trails, ideally near water or in forests. Shaded paths. Morning or late afternoon.
Play for fun, not competition. Cricket, volleyball, badminton at a relaxed pace. Avoid getting heated or angry.
Winter sports are excellent for Pitta — cooling and exhilarating.
Moderate weights, moderate reps. Focus on form over heavy lifting. Avoid ego-driven lifting.
Expressive, joyful dance. Avoid overly competitive dance forms.

avoid: Exercising in hot sun or midday heat

kapha.principle: Vigorous, stimulating, varied. Exercise to 80-90% capacity. Build a sweat. Variety prevents boredom. This is the MOST important lifestyle practice for Kapha.
kapha.bestTiming: Early morning (6-7 AM) — exercising during Kapha time (6-10 AM) directly counters Kapha's heaviness. This is the ideal window.
kapha.capacityRule: Push until you're sweating well and breathing hard. Kapha should feel energized and light after exercise. If you're not sweating, you're not doing enough.

recommended.details: Vigorous running. Interval training. Build stamina progressively. Run in different environments for variety.
Dynamic, flowing, heat-building yoga. Surya Namaskar (10-12 rounds minimum). Backbends, inversions, strong standing poses. Fast pace.
The best modern exercise for Kapha. Short bursts of intense effort. Burns fat, builds metabolism, creates lightness.
Dynamic, stimulating, builds discipline and strength. Excellent for breaking Kapha inertia.
Heavy weights, moderate reps. Build lean muscle mass. Circuit training for cardio benefit. Compound movements.
Fast-paced dance: Zumba, Bollywood dance, aerobic dance. Keeps it fun while building a sweat.
Vigorous cycling, hill climbs, spinning classes. High intensity preferred.
Football, basketball, tennis, squash — competitive sports motivate Kapha to push harder.
Challenging trails, steep inclines. The effort and variety break Kapha stagnation beautifully.

avoid: Sedentary lifestyle (the worst thing for Kapha)
Skipping exercise due to laziness or weather
Walking only (too gentle for primary exercise — fine as supplement)
Excessive swimming (water element increases Kapha — use sparingly)
Slow, passive practices without heat-building elements
